summary: Cathy O'Neil shares updates on her recent work covering algorithmic harms across multiple platforms. She announces a Bloomberg column warning that UK algorithmic grading scandals are harbingers of worse to come, a New York Times book review on gender in mathematics, and her appearance in the Netflix documentary 'The Social Dilemma.' She also highlights related work by Meredith Broussard on algorithmic grading and Yael Eisenstein on Facebook's polarization business model.
key_points: UK algorithmic grading scandals (IB and A-levels) represent the beginning of a larger wave of harmful algorithms in education
O'Neil characterizes problematic algorithms as a 'mutant army' that will increasingly affect students
The post connects her ongoing work across multiple media platforms (Bloomberg, NYT, Netflix) to a consistent theme of algorithmic accountability
She amplifies allied voices working on similar issues of algorithmic harm and tech platform manipulation
money_quotes: the recent algorithmic grading scandals in the UK are just the beginning of an oncoming mutant army of crap algorithms
key_insight: The UK algorithmic grading failures aren't isolated incidents but early warnings of a coming wave of poorly designed algorithms that will increasingly harm students and educational systems.
